architecture.summary: Chameleon is a plug-and-play compositional reasoning framework that augments LLMs with an inventory of external tools (vision models, web search, knowledge retrieval, Python execution, heuristic modules). An LLM-based planner generates natural-language-like sequential programs that compose these tools without training. Each module executes in sequence, reading the query and cached results from prior steps, updating both for downstream modules, and finally producing a task-specific answer.

biblio.title: Chameleon: Plug-and-Play Compositional Reasoning with Large Language Models

claims.statement: Chameleon powered by GPT-4 achieves an 86.54% overall accuracy on ScienceQA, improving the best published few-shot result by 11.37%
On TabMWP, GPT-4-powered Chameleon improves the accuracy by 17.0%, lifting the state of the art to 98.78%
Chameleon (ChatGPT) achieves 79.93% accuracy on ScienceQA, a 1.62% improvement over Chain-of-Thought prompted ChatGPT
Chameleon (ChatGPT) outperforms ChatGPT CoT and ChatGPT PoT on TabMWP by 11.25% and 3.79%, reaching 93.28% accuracy

GPT-4 planner exhibits more consistent and rational tool selection via inferring potential constraints from instructions, compared to ChatGPT planner; GPT-4 calls Knowledge Retrieval more frequently (81% vs. 72%) and Bing Search more (11% vs. 3%) on ScienceQA

Knowledge Retrieval plays a vital role in both ScienceQA and TabMWP tasks; domain-specific tools such as search engines and vision models for ScienceQA and program tools for TabMWP also prove important

Inaccurate responses may arise from the limitations of the current modules or from suboptimal programs generated by the planner. The module inventory may lack tools capable of addressing specific abilities.
Chameleon generates natural-language-like programs without requiring any training or carefully curated rules, prompted only by tool descriptions and usage examples in few-shot setup
